explanation: Ceramide EOP (ceramide 1) is a long-chain ceramide esterified with linoleic acid at the omega position, making it critical for forming the covalently bound lipid envelope that anchors the outermost layer of the stratum corneum. Topical ceramide EOP helps restore this structural scaffold, reducing water loss and barrier permeability in dry, eczema-prone, and reactive skin; it is exceptionally well-tolerated.

Ceramide NP (ceramide 3) is the most abundant ceramide subtype in healthy human skin, playing a central role in the lamellar lipid bilayer that maintains barrier integrity and prevents transepidermal water loss. Topical ceramide NP replenishes depleted barrier lipids to reduce dryness, sensitivity, and irritant penetration, and is exceptionally well-tolerated — particularly beneficial for eczema-prone and reactive skin.

Ceramides are a class of sphingolipids that form roughly 50% of the stratum corneum's lipid content, functioning as the mortar between skin cells to prevent water loss and block irritant entry. Topically applied ceramide blends replenish depleted barrier lipids and are exceptionally well-tolerated — most effective when formulated alongside cholesterol and fatty acids to mimic the skin's natural lipid ratio.

Ceresin is a refined mineral wax derived from naturally occurring ozokerite, used in skincare as a structuring agent and thickener in balms, sticks, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered inert and well-tolerated, providing a firm, smooth texture without the allergenic concerns associated with some plant-derived waxes.

Cereus grandiflorus (night-blooming cereus) flower extract is a cactus-derived botanical antioxidant containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect skin from oxidative stress. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its free-radical-scavenging activity.

Ceteareth-20 is a PEG-20 ether of cetearyl alcohol used as a nonionic emulsifier in oil-in-water creams and lotions, helping to create stable, smooth-textured emulsions. It is generally well-tolerated at typical formula concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Ceteareth-60 myristyl glycol is a specialized PEG-modified emulsifier used to create stable, lightweight emulsions with a smooth skin feel. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Cetearyl alcohol is a blend of cetyl (C16) and stearyl (C18) fatty alcohols derived from plant or synthetic sources, used as an emollient, emulsifier co-agent, and thickener that gives moisturizers and creams their smooth, rich texture. Unlike short-chain alcohols, cetearyl alcohol does not strip the skin barrier — it is well-tolerated and non-irritating across all skin types including sensitive and reactive.

Cetearyl dimethicone/vinyl dimethicone crosspolymer is a crosslinked silicone elastomer that creates a soft-focus, velvety skin feel and is used as a sensory modifier and film-former in primers and lightweight moisturizers. It is well-tolerated and provides a smooth, non-greasy finish with low comedogenic potential.

Cetearyl glucoside is a mild, sugar-based emulsifier derived from cetearyl alcohol and glucose, used to create stable oil-in-water emulsions in natural and clean-formula skincare. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is often combined with cetearyl alcohol (as the Emulsiplex or similar systems) to produce gentle, skin-compatible emulsions.

Cetearyl isononanoate is a lightweight synthetic ester emollient derived from cetearyl alcohol and isononanoic acid, used to provide a dry, silky skin feel without greasiness. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and particularly suited to light moisturizers and serums for oily and combination skin types.

Cetearyl olivate is an ester of cetearyl alcohol and olive oil fatty acids, used as a gentle, skin-compatible emulsifier in natural-positioned formulas. It is part of the Olivem emulsifier system, known for forming lamellar liquid crystal structures that mimic the skin's own lipid organization, and is well-tolerated by sensitive skin.

Cetyl alcohol is a C16 long-chain fatty alcohol derived from coconut or palm oil, used as an emollient, emulsifier co-agent, and thickener that gives creams and lotions their characteristic smooth, spreadable texture. Unlike short-chain alcohols, it does not dry or irritate — it is well-tolerated by all skin types including sensitive and reactive, and helps reinforce the skin's surface lipid layer.

Cetyl dimethicone is a cetyl-modified silicone that functions as both an emollient and a water-in-silicone emulsifier, providing a smooth, silky skin feel and helping to stabilize silicone-based formulas. It is well-tolerated and produces lighter, drier textures than standard dimethicone in finished formulas.

Cetyl ethylhexanoate is a lightweight synthetic ester emollient derived from cetyl alcohol and ethylhexanoic acid, providing a smooth, non-greasy skin feel with good spreadability. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and commonly used in serums and lightweight moisturizers as a skin-feel enhancer.

Cetyl PEG/PPG-10/1 dimethicone is a PEG/PPG-modified silicone emulsifier used primarily in water-in-silicone sunscreens and lightweight foundations, enabling a fast-absorbing, dry-finish texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Cetyl PEG/PPG-10/1 dimethicone is a PEG/PPG-modified silicone emulsifier used primarily in water-in-silicone sunscreens and lightweight foundations, enabling a fast-absorbing, dry-finish texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Chamaecyparis obtusa (hinoki cypress) leaf extract is a Japanese botanical antioxidant containing hinokitiol and flavonoids with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and clarifying formulas for its ability to neutralize oxidative stress and inhibit acne-causing bacteria.

Chamomilla recutita (German chamomile) flower extract is rich in bisabolol, apigenin, and chamazulene with clinically documented anti-inflammatory and skin-calming properties. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and used in soothing formulas for reactive and sensitive skin — notably the extract is significantly less sensitizing than the concentrated essential oil form.

Chamomilla recutita (German chamomile) flower extract contains bisabolol, apigenin, and chamazulene with well-documented anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and used in calming formulas for reactive and sensitive skin — the extract form is considerably less sensitizing than the concentrated chamomile essential oil.

Chamomilla recutita matricaria (German chamomile) flower extract provides bisabolol, apigenin, and chamazulene with anti-inflammatory and soothing benefits. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and used in calming formulas for reactive and sensitive skin — the extract form is considerably less sensitizing than concentrated chamomile essential oil.

Charcoal powder (activated charcoal) is a finely milled porous carbon material used in face masks and cleansers for its ability to adsorb sebum, surface impurities, and certain pore-clogging substances. It is well-tolerated in rinse-off formulas across skin types, with the detoxifying and oil-absorbing effect most beneficial for oily and congestion-prone skin.

Chenopodium quinoa (quinoa) seed extract is a botanical antioxidant derived from the superfood grain, rich in amino acids, saponins, and polyphenols that condition skin and protect against oxidative stress. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its antioxidant and skin-nourishing properties.

Chlorella minutissima extract is a microalgae extract from a freshwater green alga, rich in chlorophyll, carotenoids, and amino acids with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and support skin vitality.

Chlorella vulgaris extract is derived from a nutrient-dense freshwater green alga, providing chlorophyll, carotenoids, B vitamins, and amino acids with antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and anti-aging formulas for its ability to protect against environmental oxidative stress and support skin renewal.

Chlorphenesin is a synthetic antimicrobial preservative used at low concentrations to inhibit gram-positive bacteria and fungi in skincare formulas, often in combination with other preservatives for broader-spectrum protection. It is considered well-tolerated at typical use levels and is a common choice in formulas positioned as paraben-free.

Cholesterol is a skin-identical lipid and one of three key components of the stratum corneum's lamellar lipid bilayer (alongside ceramides and free fatty acids) that maintains barrier integrity and prevents transepidermal water loss. Topically applied cholesterol helps restore a depleted or disrupted barrier and is exceptionally well-tolerated — most effective in formulas that combine it with ceramides and fatty acids in a physiological ratio.

Chondrus crispus (Irish moss) is a red seaweed used in skincare for its high carrageenan polysaccharide content, which provides thickening, hydrating, and soothing benefits when applied to the skin. It is well-tolerated and used as both a functional thickener and a hydrating marine botanical in natural-positioned formulas.

Chondrus crispus (Irish moss) extract delivers sulfated polysaccharides (carrageenans) from red seaweed with hydrating, soothing, and mild antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated and used in marine-ingredient formulas as a calming and moisture-retaining skin conditioner.

Chondrus crispus (Irish moss) extract provides sulfated polysaccharides from red seaweed with hydrating, soothing, and mild antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated and used in marine-ingredient formulas as a calming skin conditioner that helps retain surface moisture.
